title: phacelia californica f. bernardina
intro: Phacelia californica f. bernardina, also known as California blue-cups, is a flowering plant species in the borage family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Baja California. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily its demulcent and antiseptic qualities, which can soothe and protect the mucous membranes, skin, and wounds. The health benefits of Phacelia californica f. bernardina include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and provide antioxidant protection. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, allowing for topical and internal use to harness its medicinal properties.
